response: The descendants of the group of black Indians associated with the Seminole people settled in distinct bands near the Native American Seminole, primarily in Florida and Oklahoma. These groups, known as Black Seminoles, were descendants of free blacks and escaped slaves (called maroons) who allied with Seminole groups in Spanish Florida. They lived in separate bands and had more freedom than slaves held by whites in the South or by other Native American tribes, including the right to bear arms.
